title: OccAny: Generalized Unconstrained Urban 3D Occupancy
summary: OccAny addresses the generalization limitations of existing 3D urban occupancy prediction models by creating the first unconstrained framework capable of handling out-of-domain, uncalibrated sensor inputs (monocular, sequential, or surround-view). The method introduces two key technical components: Segmentation Forcing, which leverages mask predictions to improve volumetric quality, and a Novel View Rendering pipeline for geometry completion via test-time augmentation. Experiments show OccAny surpasses visual geometry baselines while maintaining competitiveness against specialized in-domain self-supervised models on standard urban occupancy benchmarks. This work successfully bridges general visual geometry capabilities with the specific requirements of metric and semantic prediction in complex urban environments.

title: Active learning-enabled multi-objective design of thermally conductive and mechanically compliant polymers
summary: This work introduces an active learning (AL) framework leveraging multi-objective Bayesian optimization (MOBO) to accelerate the discovery of polymers optimized for both high thermal conductivity (TC) and mechanical compliance (low bulk modulus). The methodology trains independent Deep Kernel Learning (DKL) models as surrogate predictors for these properties and their uncertainties, using an initial dataset generated by a high-throughput molecular dynamics (MD) pipeline. The framework iteratively uses the parallel noisy expected hypervolume improvement (qNEHVI) acquisition function to recommend the most informative candidates for further MD simulation and model updating. This approach successfully identified six Pareto-optimal polymer candidates and provided insights into the underlying molecular feature trade-offs, significantly streamlining the development cycle for multifunctional materials.

title: Foveated Diffusion: Efficient Spatially Adaptive Image and Video Generation
summary: This work addresses the quadratic complexity bottleneck in high-resolution image and video diffusion models by introducing Foveated Diffusion, which exploits the non-uniform acuity of human vision based on known gaze location. The method assigns a higher density of tokens to the foveal region where detail is critical and a lower density to the periphery. A novel mechanism is proposed for constructing these mixed-resolution tokens directly from high-resolution inputs, allowing existing diffusion models to be efficiently post-trained. This results in generation speeds and token counts vastly reduced compared to full-resolution generation while maintaining perceptual quality, validated by both technical metrics and human studies.

title: Dynamic Light Spanners in Doubling Metrics
summary: This paper addresses the challenge of dynamically maintaining a $(1+\varepsilon)$-spanner for a point set $X$ undergoing insertions and deletions within a metric space of constant doubling dimension. The authors introduce an efficient algorithm where the total weight of the resulting spanner is kept within a constant factor of the Minimum Spanning Tree (MST) weight. Crucially, both insertion and deletion operations are processed in polylogarithmic time with respect to the aspect ratio $\Phi$ of the point set. This work resolves an open problem by providing the first efficient dynamic algorithm for maintaining light-weight spanners, even in settings like low-dimensional Euclidean space.

title: One View Is Enough! Monocular Training for In-the-Wild Novel View Generation
summary: The paper introduces OVIE, a novel approach to monocular novel view synthesis that eliminates the need for multi-view supervision by training exclusively on large-scale, unpaired internet images. The core idea involves using a monocular depth estimator to scaffold the training process by lifting the source view into 3D, applying a transformation, and projecting a pseudo-target view. To manage noise from the unstructured data, a masked training formulation restricts various loss functions to valid projection regions, allowing scaling to 30 million images. Crucially, the resulting OVIE model is geometry-free at inference, achieving superior zero-shot performance while being significantly faster than previous methods.

title: Stablecoins as Dry Powder: A Copula-Based Risk Analysis of Cryptocurrency Markets
summary: This study investigates the systemic role of stablecoins in the cryptocurrency ecosystem by quantifying the transmission of risk and activity from stablecoin markets to broader digital asset markets using copula-based methods. The authors establish in-sample causality across multiple forecasting horizons and show that stablecoin volume and upside volatility act as critical leading indicators, effectively functioning as 'dry powder' for market movements. Incorporating these stablecoin factors significantly improves forecasting accuracy, as evidenced by reduced Mean Squared Error in cryptocurrency predictions. Furthermore, the practical economic value is demonstrated by showing that using these factors in a volatility targeting strategy leads to overall risk reduction.

title: Statistical Efficiency of Single- and Multi-step Models for Forecasting and Control
summary: This paper quantitatively analyzes the trade-off between using single-step and multi-step predictors for forecasting and subsequent closed-loop control, particularly in the context of compounding error in learning-based control. The authors investigate three predictor classes applied to linear dynamical systems: single-step, direct multi-step, and single-step models trained with multi-step losses. They theoretically establish that if the system dynamics are perfectly specified, single-step models yield the lowest asymptotic prediction error. However, in the presence of model misspecification due to partial observability, direct multi-step predictors offer significant advantages in reducing bias and improving overall accuracy, a benefit that extends to practical control scenarios.

title: PNap: Lifecycle-aware Edge Multi-state sleep for Energy Efficient MEC
summary: This paper addresses the high energy consumption in Multi-access Edge Computing (MEC) systems caused by keeping multiple servers active during low-load periods. The authors introduce PowerNap (PNap), a novel orchestration framework designed to jointly manage server multi-state sleep mechanisms and service lifecycle operations to ensure service continuity. PNap utilizes traffic forecasting to intelligently decide when to transition servers into deeper sleep states, thereby minimizing the number of active servers while avoiding service disruptions from slow wake-up times. Experimental results demonstrate that PNap achieves up to 14.9% energy savings compared to a state-of-the-art approach while achieving equivalent service availability metrics.

title: Granger Causality in Expectiles: an M-vine copula test
summary: This paper introduces a novel, model-free measure for Granger causality operating in the space of expectiles, extending the concept beyond the mean to study distributional causality at any point of the conditional distribution. To test for multivariate Granger causality involving $d+1$ series under general non-linear and non-Gaussian dependency, the authors develop a test statistic founded on M-vine copula models, thereby avoiding parametric assumptions on the underlying joint distribution. The strong consistency of this test statistic is proven, and simulations confirm accurate size control and increasing power with sample size. A significant advantage of this framework is its capacity for joint hypothesis testing, successfully identifying causal structures invisible to standard pairwise analyses.

title: WaveSFNet: A Wavelet-Based Codec and Spatial--Frequency Dual-Domain Gating Network for Spatiotemporal Prediction
summary: WaveSFNet is an efficient recurrent-free framework proposed for spatiotemporal prediction, addressing the challenge of preserving high-frequency details during downsampling. It integrates a novel wavelet-based codec to retain high-frequency subband cues, which is coupled with a spatial--frequency dual-domain gated translator. This translator enhances dynamics by incorporating adjacent-frame differences and fuses large-kernel spatial modeling with frequency-domain global modulation. Experiments on datasets like Moving MNIST, TaxiBJ, and WeatherBench demonstrate WaveSFNet achieves competitive accuracy while maintaining low computational overhead.
